title: arundinella rupestris var. pachyathera
intro: Arundinella rupestris var. pachyathera, also known as Arundinella pachyathera, is a type of grass native to the Himalayan region, which belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which help to combat various health issues. The health benefits of Arundinella pachyathera include reducing fever, treating skin infections, and soothing digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Arundinella pachyathera include decoctions, infusions, and powder, which can be used to make teas, capsules, or topical creams for various health benefits.
